reddit_title: Spinal cord injury from a car crash in Texas — what legal steps actually matter in the first few weeks?
reddit_body:
If you or someone you know has suffered a spinal cord injury in a car crash in Irving or anywhere in Texas, the legal timeline starts immediately — even while you're focused on medical care. Texas has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but critical evidence like dashcam footage, traffic camera recordings, and witness accounts can disappear within days. Sending a spoliation letter to preserve evidence early can make or break a case.

For severe injuries like spinal cord damage, the value of a claim goes far beyond immediate medical bills. Attorneys experienced in catastrophic injury cases will work with life care planners and economists to document lifetime costs — ongoing rehabilitation, adaptive housing, lost earning capacity, and pain and suffering. Insurance companies know this and will often try to settle fast and low before you understand the full picture.

If you're unsure where to start finding an attorney who actually handles these types of cases, wreckmatch.com is a free matching service that connects injury survivors with personal injury lawyers who specialize in severe and catastrophic cases. No pressure, no cost to get matched — just a faster way to find the right legal help when time matters most.
